best_reference_text: Smoking is a major cause of lung cancer and significantly increases cancer risk.

claim: Vaccination reduces the risk of infectious disease
best_reference_text: Vaccines help prevent infectious diseases and reduce severe illness.

claim: Type 2 diabetes leads to high blood sugar
best_reference_text: Type 2 diabetes causes blood sugar levels to become too high.

claim: Regular exercise helps lower blood pressure
best_reference_text: Regular exercise lowers blood pressure and improves cardiovascular health.

claim: Obesity increases the risk of heart disease
best_reference_text: Obesity increases the risk of serious conditions including heart disease.
Alcohol is classified as a carcinogen and increases the risk of several cancers.
A healthy diet supports overall health and helps the body function properly.
Physical activity can help improve mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety.
Healthy fats contribute to cardiovascular health as part of a balanced diet.
Long-term stress can affect the body and contribute to health problems.
Vaccination helps reduce disease spread and protects vulnerable groups.
Eating a low-fat diet with fibre can help lower cholesterol and heart disease risk.
Cancer is caused by complex genetic and environmental factors and requires evidence-based treatment.
Vaccines are effective in preventing infectious disease and no reliable evidence supports this claim.
COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus and spreads through respiratory droplets.

claim: Pregnancy can be cured with antibiotics
best_reference_text: Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections and are not a treatment for pregnancy.

claim: Alcohol improves memory and brain performance
best_reference_text: Alcohol affects brain chemistry and can negatively affect mental and physical health.

claim: Weight loss surgery is the same as vaccination
best_reference_text: Vaccination prevents infectious disease, while surgery is a medical procedure used for obesity treatment.
